title: Conditional compiler definitions for CMake build always set
body:
Running a pretty old testbench with a current release of verilator and CMAKE, I noticed that the compile definitions for the target are now always set to 0 or 1. Previously, they were only set if the corresponding target property was true. This causes issues with my testbench, which relies on these definitions to be set conditionally.

The affected definitions are:
- VM_COVERAGE
- VM_SC
- VM_TRACE
- VM_TRACE_VCD
- VM_TRACE_FST
- VM_TRACE_SAIF

This is breaking legacy SystemC/C++ code of the form:
#ifdef VM_TRACE
    // some code
#endif

Where the VM_TRACE definition is always set (set to 1 or 0).

Current behaviour generates the following compiler command line:

/usr/bin/c++ -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=1 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -DVM_TRACE_SAIF=0 -I/home/... ...

Expected Behaviour:
/usr/bin/c++ -DVM_SC -I/home/... ...

I don't know if this is the intended behavior from the Verilator team, but it seems at odds with standard practice.


What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

CMake based design using the following verilate call:

verilator_link_systemc(simple)
verilate(simple
        TOP_MODULE
            crc16
        PREFIX "HDL_CRC"
        SYSTEMC
        INCLUDE_DIRS
            hdl
        #TRACE_FST  <- The offending code when commented off
        SOURCES
        ../../hdl/crc/crc16.sv
)

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  
    git master version

What OS and distribution are you using?
    Ubuntu 24.04 on AMD64
